title: juncus novae-zelandiae
intro: Juncus novae-zelandiae, also known as New Zealand rush or New Zealand hair grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal and health benefits, with properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin irritations, and its health benefits include soothing and calming effects, as well as promoting wound healing. Juncus novae-zelandiae is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
